_comment: Per-source and per-event attention routing rules. Applied after base level calculation in AttentionClassifier.

_levels.2: notable — task completed, user asked to be notified
_levels.1: background note — worth logging, not worth interrupting

source_rules._comment: Fallback per-source min/max caps. Only applied when no event_rule matches.

dreaming._comment: Memory consolidation is background work. Never interrupt the user for it.

assistant._comment: Role-based assistant tasks: normal rules apply. waiting_for_input=4, failures=3, notify completions=2.

agent._comment: External coding agents (OpenCode, etc): failures and completions are always worth surfacing.

scheduled._comment: Calendar/cron tasks: notify on completion if the task asked, otherwise quiet.

event_rules._comment: Dreaming failures are background noise — cap at 1, never surface as a full error.
Security sentinel failures are always critical — override any source cap.
Coding agent failures are at least error-level (3), not just notable (2).
Coding agent HITL questions surface as blocking (4), not capped by agent min_level.
Scheduled task failures are notable (2), not full errors (3) — cron noise.
